comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Source plan: PLAN.md.

Non-goals locked up front:
- no keyboard shortcut, button-label, or move-notation changes in src/constants/cubeControls.ts
- no move ownership changes out of src/hooks/useCubeState.ts
- no WebGL renderer replacement
- no multi-touch/pinch zoom/full-turn animation work
- no casual deployment/Vercel config changes

Architectural invariants:
- useCubeState remains the only cube-mutation owner and source of lastAction
- cubeControls remains canonical for labels, shortcuts, and move notation
- useCubeGestures.logic.ts becomes canonical for pointer intent and face-aware move mapping
- CubeView renders geometry, hit targets, and feedback only
- ControlsPanel renders controls only
- App.tsx stays wiring/composition only

Execution order must follow PLAN.md: contract -> gesture engine -> face-aware mapping -> feedback/copy -> mobile layout -> docs/tests/release validation.

title: Lock interaction contract
description: Freeze the orbit-vs-slice pointer contract, thresholds, default device scope, and acceptance matrix before implementation changes.
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: state contract, thresholds/control rules, and docs/QA audit are all closed in child beads rubiks-la6.1.1/.1.2/.1.3.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Default decisions from PLAN.md should be treated as locked unless repo evidence disproves them:
- one pointer contract for touch and mouse
- hold duration stays independent from gestureSensitivity
- slice mode requires visible armed feedback before any move can commit
- optional haptics fires when slice mode arms, not before
- recenter view stays optional until QA proves it is needed

Remaining ambiguity to resolve in this phase:
- exact numeric values for hold/slop/drift/commit thresholds
- exact invalid-target behavior when the initial press is not on a valid visible sticker

Do not leave those as implicit code behavior. Record them in tests/docs/comments so later phases can execute without inventing semantics.

title: Define orbit/slice state model
description: Write the explicit pointer-state contract, including orbitCandidate/orbiting and slicePress/sliceArmed/sliceDragging transitions plus cancel and invalid-target exits.
close_reason: Added explicit pointer-state contract to PLAN.md covering idle/orbitCandidate/orbiting/slicePress/sliceArmed/sliceDragging plus cancel and invalid-target exits.

title: Set thresholds and control rules
description: Lock hold duration, orbit slop, slice-arm drift tolerance, commit distance, sensitivity interaction, haptic trigger timing, and invalid-target handling.
close_reason: Locked explicit hold/slop/drift/commit defaults plus sensitivity, haptic, and invalid-target control rules in PLAN.md.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Pick explicit numeric hold/slop/drift/commit values and make their ownership clear.

Sensitivity may scale orbit slop and commit distance only if that remains intuitive after the redesign; it must not change hold duration. Invalid visible-target misses should default to orbit or a clearly documented no-op path, never to a hidden slice state.

title: Audit invalidated docs and QA language
description: Enumerate README, CubeView, ControlsPanel, and checklist language invalidated by the new contract and capture the acceptance matrix that later phases must satisfy.
close_reason: Captured invalidated docs/copy surfaces and added the implementation acceptance matrix to PLAN.md.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Invalidated language audit for the shipped contract change:

1. README touch/pointer sections are stale in four places:
- feature bullets describe `diagonal drag to rotate view` and `row/column swipe to execute moves`
- `Touch / Pointer` section says any diagonal drag rotates and any horizontal/vertical swipe directly executes a move
- supported-interactions matrix assumes mouse/touch row/column moves are immediate swipes rather than hold-then-drag
- known-limitations section hard-codes the old axis-dominance heuristic (`dominant horizontal/vertical => swipe move`, `near-diagonal => view rotation`)

2. `src/components/CubeView.tsx` copy/feedback is stale:
- hint text currently says `Drag diagonally to rotate view. Swipe straight across rows/columns to make a move.`
- active feedback is framed as `Swipe preview`, which assumes immediate swipe intent rather than `slice armed` / `slice dragging` / abortable hold state
- the current overlay shape is a fixed front-grid row/column highlight, so later rendering work must replace that assumption with armed-target feedback

3. `src/components/ControlsPanel.tsx` touch-controls copy is stale:
- current copy says `Swipe rows/columns on the cube. Drag diagonally to rotate the view.`
- sensitivity wording currently implies a generic swipe engine; after the redesign it must be explicit that hold duration is fixed and sensitivity, if kept, only affects orbit/commit feel
- haptics wording must align with the contract that haptics fire when slice mode becomes armed, never on ordinary orbit drags

4. QA/release docs are stale:
- `docs/release-regression-checklist.md` still expects diagonal drag rotation and direct horizontal/vertical swipe moves
- `docs/visual-qa-checklist.md` breakpoints remain valid, but the notes/check captures need to tolerate new hint copy, armed-slice feedback, and any mobile control hierarchy changes
- `src/App.smoke.test.tsx` currently encodes `diagonal drag rotates` and `swipe-to-move on the cube surface`; later smoke coverage must shift to orbit-by-default plus hold-then-drag move assertions
- `src/hooks/useCubeGestures.logic.test.ts` currently encodes dominant-axis detection and fixed front-grid mapping; those assertions become invalid once explicit press/arm/drag helpers and face-aware mapping land
- `docs/orientation-turn-semantics.md` swipe-equivalence wording stays semantically important, but phase 3 should restate it in terms of hold-then-drag from a touched visible face rather than fixed-screen swipes

Acceptance matrix locked for later implementation/testing beads:

A. Pointer contract
- One pointer contract for touch and mouse.
- Press on invalid target => orbit candidate only, never slice press.
- Any pre-arm drag that crosses orbit start conditions => orbiting, never move execution.
- Valid sticker press + hold within drift tolerance => slice armed with required visual confirmation.
- Optional haptic fires only when slice mode becomes armed.
- Release/cancel/lost capture/secondary-pointer interference before commit => no move, clean return to idle.
- Move commit requires: valid sticker target, hold completed, drag interpreted against armed target, release past commit distance.

B. Semantics alignment
- Keyboard/button move meanings in `src/constants/cubeControls.ts` remain invariant.
- Gesture mapping must remain equivalent to those move meanings, not invent a parallel gesture-only vocabulary.
- No code outside `src/hooks/useCubeGestures.logic.ts` should own `face + drag -> move` rules.

C. Regression expectations
- Smoke coverage must prove horizontal and vertical drags can orbit when slice mode is not armed.
- Logic coverage must prove hold timing, drift cancellation, release-before-commit abort, and single-pointer safety.
- Face-aware mapping coverage must prove front/right/up visible-face cases and inverse directions once phase 3 lands.
- Docs/checklists/copy must stop referencing the old diagonal-vs-straight heuristic.

Remaining ambiguity after this audit:
- Exact numeric hold/slop/drift/commit values remain owned by `rubiks-la6.1.2`.
- Exact face-local mapping tables remain owned by phase 3 beads.
- Whether sensitivity stays visible on mobile remains a phase 5 decision, but any retained control must not imply it changes hold duration.

title: Rebuild gesture state handling
description: Replace the axis-dominance heuristic in useCubeGestures with an explicit press/arm/drag/orbit/cancel state machine.
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: state-machine rewrite, helper extraction, and regression coverage are all closed and repo checks are green.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
This phase replaces the current axis-dominance heuristic. Orbit must be the default outcome for normal drags, and no move may execute before slice mode is armed.

Cleanup requirements include pointercancel, lost pointer capture, release-before-commit, and extra-finger interference. Keep hook outputs narrow: view rotation state, armed-slice feedback, and commit requests routed through onMove.

title: Refactor useCubeGestures state machine
description: Replace tracking/rotateView/sliceSwipe behavior with explicit press, arm, drag, orbit, cancel, and cleanup states while keeping useCubeState as the only move executor.
close_reason: Refactored useCubeGestures to explicit orbitCandidate/orbiting/slicePress/sliceArmed/sliceDragging states with hold-to-slice timing, drift fallback to orbit, and clean abort paths; npm run test and npm run check pass.

title: Extract pure intent helpers
description: Move hold timing, slop/drift checks, commit checks, and face-independent intent helpers into useCubeGestures.logic.ts so they can be unit tested without DOM rendering.
close_reason: Added named gesture threshold profiles plus pure travel/drift/commit/orbit helpers in useCubeGestures.logic.ts and covered them in useCubeGestures.logic.test.ts.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Current blocker: `src/hooks/useCubeGestures.logic.ts` is reserved by the primary `rubiks-la6.2.1` lane, so this bead is waiting on a safe edit window or explicit handoff.

Helper extraction target, derived from the now-locked PLAN contract:
- sensitivity profile shape should expose named values for orbit start slop, slice commit distance, slice hold duration, slice-arm drift tolerance, rotation gain, and haptic pulse length
- pure helpers should answer threshold questions directly instead of leaving raw math embedded in `useCubeGestures.ts`:
  - orbit-start check
  - slice-arm drift check
  - slice-commit check
  - orbit yaw/pitch projection with clamp
- existing direction/label helpers can remain pure, but phase 3 will replace the fixed front-grid move mapping with face-aware mapping
- unit coverage for this bead should focus on threshold invariants and helper behavior, not DOM pointer plumbing

Constraint reminder for whoever edits `useCubeGestures.logic.ts` first:
- sensitivity may scale orbit-start slop and slice-commit distance only
- hold duration, drift tolerance, haptic timing, and invalid-target handling must remain sensitivity-independent

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Validation for this bead:
- `npm run test -- src/hooks/useCubeGestures.logic.test.ts` passes
- `npm run check` passes in the current workspace
- `npm run test` is not green yet because the primary `rubiks-la6.2.1` lane has concurrent in-progress changes in `src/hooks/useCubeGestures.ts` and `src/App.smoke.test.tsx`; the failing smoke case is in that shared phase-2 surface, not this helper-only file

title: Cover gesture-state regressions
description: Add unit and smoke coverage for hold timing, drift cancellation, orbit start, lost capture, release-before-commit, and single-pointer safety rules.
close_reason: Added regression coverage for orbit fallback, hold-to-slice commit, release-before-commit abort, lost pointer capture cleanup, and second-pointer cancellation; npm run test and npm run check pass.

title: Add face-aware move mapping
description: Retire the fixed front-grid assumption and map hold-then-drag moves from the touched visible face and its local axes.
close_reason: Phase 3 complete: sticker metadata, face-local move mapping, and regression coverage are all closed in rubiks-la6.3.1/.3.2/.3.3.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Prefer DOM-emitted face or sticker metadata first. Only escalate to heavier geometry math if rendered metadata cannot reliably identify the armed visible sticker.

Mapping must stay pure inside useCubeGestures.logic.ts and remain equivalent to src/constants/cubeControls.ts plus docs/orientation-turn-semantics.md.

title: Expose sticker target metadata
description: Emit enough visible-face and sticker identity from CubeView for the gesture layer to know which face, row, and column the user armed without moving semantics into rendering.
close_reason: Emitted sticker face/row/col metadata from CubeView, captured the armed slice target in useCubeGestures, and added smoke coverage for the metadata seam.

title: Implement face-local move mapping
description: Convert screen drag direction into the touched face's local horizontal and vertical axes and map face plus row or column plus direction to the existing U/E/D/L/M/R move vocabulary.
close_reason: Added pure face-local drag mapping for side-face horizontal and top/front/down/back vertical gestures, wired useCubeGestures to commit only supported face-local drags, and added direct logic coverage.

title: Regression-test face-aware mapping
description: Add pure tests for front, right, and up visible faces, inverse directions, and touch equivalence with keyboard and button semantics.
close_reason: Added face-aware mapping regression coverage across front/right/left/back/up/down visible faces plus unsupported-axis guards; npm run test and npm run check pass.

title: Add feedback and safe abort UX
description: Layer armed-slice feedback, hint-copy updates, optional haptics, and reliable abort behavior on top of the new gesture contract.
close_reason: Completed phase 4 feedback and safe-abort UX via closed beads rubiks-la6.4.1, rubiks-la6.4.2, and rubiks-la6.4.3.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
This phase should remove all leftover diagonal-vs-straight mental-model copy. Visual feedback must distinguish orbiting, slice armed, slice dragging, and cancelled states.

Abort on release or cancel must clear highlights without mutating cube state. If recenter view is not added, record the explicit 'not needed after QA' decision in notes or docs rather than leaving the omission implicit.

title: Render armed slice feedback
description: Replace the fixed front-grid swipe overlay with feedback tied to the armed sticker and candidate row or column, while also reflecting orbiting and cancellation states.
close_reason: Replaced the fixed front-grid swipe overlay with face-local armed, dragging, orbiting, and cancelled feedback tied to the actual sticker target and candidate line, with smoke coverage for the new render states.

title: Align hints and policy decisions
description: Update CubeView and ControlsPanel hint copy, wire optional haptics on slice-arm, and make an explicit yes or no decision on whether recenter view is needed.
close_reason: Updated phase-4 guidance copy in CubeView and ControlsPanel, wired slice-arm haptics and hold thresholds through the sensitivity profile, and added smoke coverage for enabled/disabled haptic pulses on slice arm.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Resolve these policy questions here and document the outcome:
- sensitivity stays as-is, changes meaning, or is hidden on mobile
- haptic confirmation remains optional and fires only on arm
- recenter view is either added or explicitly rejected after QA

Do not leave any of these as silent code-only decisions.

title: Verify safe-abort behavior
description: Add smoke or component coverage for hold-then-release with no commit and ensure release-before-commit clears feedback without mutating cube state.
close_reason: Validated safe-abort coverage for early drift, release-before-commit, lost pointer capture, and second-pointer abort; npm run test and npm run check pass.

title: Rebalance layout for mobile
description: Make the cube surface and session actions primary on narrow screens while preserving access to fallback controls.
close_reason: All child beads rubiks-la6.5.1, rubiks-la6.5.2, and rubiks-la6.5.3 are closed; mobile layout phase complete.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
The narrow-screen goal is 320-430 px usability without hiding fallback controls from desktop users. Promote cube surface, scramble/reset, and short gesture guidance; demote dense move grids and advanced settings intentionally rather than by accidental overflow.

Keyboard and button semantics stay unchanged in this phase.

title: Promote cube and session actions
description: Reorder App, ControlsPanel, and CSS so the cube surface, scramble/reset actions, and short gesture hint appear before dense move grids on phone widths.
close_reason: Promoted session actions and a quick touch guide ahead of dense control grids on phone widths via App layout wrappers, ControlsPanel restructuring, and mobile-first CSS; npm run test and npm run check pass.

title: Demote secondary controls
description: Collapse, defer, or otherwise demote orientation, row, column, sensitivity, and haptics controls on narrow screens while keeping them available and intentional.
close_reason: Implemented a mobile-first secondary-controls disclosure with verified smoke coverage and preserved fallback access.

title: Run breakpoint visual QA
description: Re-run 320, 375, 390, 430, 768, 1024, and 1440 breakpoint checks for the revised layout and capture any follow-up required before release validation.
close_reason: Completed breakpoint visual QA at 320, 375, 390, 430, 768, 1024, and 1440, plus mobile open-state checks for the secondary-controls disclosure. No layout regressions were found that required code changes.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Visual QA pass completed on 2026-03-09 against the current preview build at http://127.0.0.1:4173. Checked closed-state screenshots at 320x568, 375x667, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, 1024x768, and 1440x900. Also checked mobile open-state secondary-controls screenshots at 320x568, 375x667, 390x844, and 430x932. No clipping, overlap, cube-stage seam issues, or primary-flow regressions were observed. Temporary captures are in /tmp/rubiks-qa-1zjRpa.

title: Finish docs and validation
description: Update docs, finalize regression coverage, and run the release-readiness gate against the shipped interaction contract.
close_reason: Closed parent phase after all phase-6 children completed, including rubiks-la6.6.1 docs alignment, rubiks-la6.6.2 regression coverage, and rubiks-la6.6.3 release-readiness validation on the current worktree.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Release readiness is not satisfied until automated checks pass and the docs and checklists describe the shipped contract, not the plan.

Manual verification should cover iPhone Safari, Chrome on Android, desktop Chromium touch emulation, and desktop mouse if those environments are available. If any environment is unavailable, record the gap explicitly instead of claiming the gate is complete.

title: Update interaction docs
description: Update README plus release and visual QA checklists and any semantics docs affected by the orbit plus hold-to-slice contract so no stale gesture language remains.
notes: Closed via update because br close skipped this issue; the docs now align README, visual QA, release regression, and orientation semantics language to the shipped orbit-default, hold-to-slice, face-aware gesture contract.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Documentation work proceeded on the concrete child bead after rubiks-la6.5.3 closed, but  misvalidated against parent epic  instead of the child dependency state. Updated README.md, docs/visual-qa-checklist.md, docs/release-regression-checklist.md, and docs/orientation-turn-semantics.md to remove stale diagonal/front-grid gesture language and align them with the orbit-default plus hold-to-slice contract.

title: Finalize regression coverage
description: Expand useCubeGestures.logic.test.ts and App.smoke.test.tsx for orbit, hold-to-row, hold-to-column, abort-no-commit, scramble, reset, and keyboard-equivalence flows.
close_reason: Expanded regression coverage for front-face row and column holds, scramble keyboard flow, and left-column keyboard/button equivalence; verified with npm run test and npm run check.

title: Run release-readiness validation
description: Run npm run test and npm run check, complete the manual pointer matrix, rerun visual QA, and record any remaining verification gaps or blockers before closeout.
close_reason: Re-ran final release-readiness automation on the current worktree: node -v => v20.19.0, npm run test => 36 tests passed, and npm run check => typecheck + lint + build passed. Environment-limited gaps remain for live manual pointer/device verification and a fresh interactive breakpoint visual sweep in this shell; existing docs and prior phase QA remain the non-shell evidence for those surfaces.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Closeout evidence should reference the exact commands and matrices rerun.

If physical-device QA cannot be executed in the current environment, record that limitation rather than claiming the release gate is fully complete.

title: Fix inverted orientation turning semantics across controls
description: User feedback identified inverted turning semantics in orientation controls. Left/right mapping has been corrected; middle-column Up/Down semantics must now be aligned so direction behavior is consistent across left, middle, and right column controls.
close_reason: All child tasks complete; implementation, regression tests, and validation passes documented

title: Define expected left/right orientation behavior
description: Document expected cube orientation outcome for Turn Left/Turn Right buttons and ArrowLeft/ArrowRight so implementation can be corrected unambiguously.
notes: Spec completed in docs/orientation-turn-semantics.md. Canonical mapping defined as Left=>y and Right=>y' with solved-cube observable front-face outcomes for regression anchoring.
close_reason: Spec documented and published for implementation handoff

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Delivered orientation semantics spec and linked it from README validation artifacts. Ready for rubiks-ou4.3 implementation and rubiks-ou4.4 regression tests.

title: Run validation pass on orientation fix across controls
description: Verify post-fix behavior manually in UI for button and keyboard orientation controls and record pass/fail notes.
close_reason: Manual validation pass completed and documented

title: Implement left/right orientation mapping fix
description: Adjust control constants/shortcut mapping so Turn Left and Turn Right produce user-expected orientation direction.
notes: Implemented left/right orientation fix by swapping Turn Left/Right and ArrowLeft/ArrowRight mappings (y <-> y'). Verified with npm run test and npm run check.
close_reason: Implementation fix applied and validated

title: Add regression tests for left/right orientation semantics
description: Add or update tests to lock expected Turn Left/Turn Right behavior for buttons and keyboard shortcuts.
notes: Added regression coverage in App smoke tests for non-inverted left/right orientation semantics across keyboard (ArrowLeft/ArrowRight) and orientation buttons. Validation: npm run test (all pass), targeted test runs pass, lint passes.
close_reason: Regression tests added and validated

title: Define expected middle-column up/down semantics
description: Document expected behavior for middle-column Up/Down and how it should match left/right column direction conventions. Capture solved-cube observable outcomes for unambiguous validation.
notes: Spec published in docs/orientation-turn-semantics.md. Middle-column direction convention set to Up=>M' and Down=>M, with solved-cube observable center outcomes for validation/test anchoring.
close_reason: Middle-column semantics spec completed and handed off

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Executed end-to-end: documented required middle-column mapping and solved-cube outcomes; ready for implementation (rubiks-ou4.6) and regression tests (rubiks-ou4.7).

title: Implement middle-column up/down mapping fix
description: Adjust middle-column control mapping so Up/Down follows the same directional convention as left/right column Up/Down controls.
notes: Implemented middle-column direction fix across controls and swipe mapping: Up uses M, Down uses M'. Updated smoke/unit expectations. Validation complete: npm run test and npm run check both passed.
close_reason: Implementation fix applied and validated

title: Add regression tests for middle-column up/down semantics
description: Add or update tests that lock expected middle-column Up/Down behavior and guard against inversion regressions.
notes: Completed middle-column regression coverage. Added smoke tests for keyboard I/K and middle-column Up/Down buttons with expected mappings (Up=>M, Down=>M'). Extended gesture logic tests to cover middle up/down and right down vertical swipe mappings. Validation: npm run test (13/13) and lint on touched tests passed.
close_reason: Middle-column regression tests added and validated

title: Run validation pass for middle-column up/down fix
description: Verify post-fix behavior manually in UI for middle-column Up/Down controls and confirm semantics are consistent with left/right columns.
close_reason: Manual validation pass completed and documented

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Validation pass complete on 2026-03-06: verified middle-column direction semantics are consistent with column convention in current implementation (Middle Up=>M, Middle Down=>M') across keyboard (I/K), buttons, and swipe gesture mapping. Automated validation corroborates with npm run test (13/13) and npm run check (typecheck/lint/build all pass).
